description: Cross-meeting canonicalization companion to 002_standup_name_variants: the SAME canonical entities (account-northwind, account-brightforge, project-atlas-migration, the three delivery people) appear under DIFFERENT surface forms ('the NorthWind account', 'BRIGHT FORGE', 'Atlas'). The entities report flags when 002 and 006 extractions normalize differently.

meeting.transcript:
**Priya Raman**: Delivery review, three items: the NorthWind account, BRIGHT FORGE, and Atlas. Tom first.

**Tom Eckhart**: The Northwind migration finished Sunday night. All records loaded, the manual-review pile came in under three percent. Their steering committee saw the numbers Thursday and they're happy.

**Priya Raman**: Great outcome. Anything left on that account?

**Tom Eckhart**: Just the decommission of the legacy CRM, which their IT owns, not us. I'll stay on it as a watcher.

**Priya Raman**: Maya, BRIGHT FORGE?

**Maya Goldstein**: Demo went well. The cohort completion widget landed, time-to-certificate slipped to this sprint. Their success team already wants a third widget — funnel drop-off. I told them we'd scope it after the current two ship.

**Priya Raman**: Good call, don't let the scope creep in sideways. And Atlas — kickoff happened Monday, the schema notes Tom sent were exactly what the doc needed. First milestone is the staging environment by June 15th. Tom, you own that one.

**Tom Eckhart**: Yep, staging env by the fifteenth, it's on my board.

**Maya Goldstein**: One more thing — I'm out the last week of June, planning around the widget delivery.

**Priya Raman**: Noted. Okay: NorthWind wrapped pending decommission watch, BrightForge widget two in flight, Atlas staging by June 15th. Thanks both.

forbidden_entities.name: IT
forbidden_entities.reason: the client's IT department, not one of our teams

forbidden_entities.name: steering committee
forbidden_entities.reason: generic governance body of the client, not a tracked team
